summary_one_line: Gurmukhi-script glyph stream with codepoint 0x0A72 immediately followed by one of 0x0A3F/0x0A40/0x0A47 (a matched vowel pair) to take the dotted-circle true branch

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A glyph buffer whose script resolves to HB_SCRIPT_GURMUKHI and that contains an adjacent codepoint pair where buffer->cur()==0x0A72 and buffer->cur(1) is one of {0x0A3F,0x0A40,0x0A47}, so that `matched` becomes true at the blocker line 162.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog (winner) uniquely reaches `case 0x0A72u:` (line 144 W T=1 vs L T=0) and takes the true side of the blocker (line 162 W T=1 vs L T=0), resolving in all 10 trials, because its CMP_MAP gradient feedback rewards inputs that progressively match the two-codepoint constraint comparison.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: cmplog (loser) blocks in all 10 trials and never enters the 0x0A72 arm (line 144 L T=0); it iterates the Gurmukhi loop heavily (line 131 L T=300) but only its edge-bucket feedback fires, giving no reward for partial progress toward the exact codepoint pair, so it never assembles the matched pair.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between the two involved fuzzers is value_profile: both carry the same input-to-state CMP substitution mutator, but only value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets. The Hamming/prefix-distance value-profile feedback makes near-miss codepoint comparisons (e.g. one byte off from 0x0A72 / 0x0A3F) register as new coverage, steering the corpus toward the exact matched vowel pair that cmplog's edge-only feedback cannot distinguish from any other non-matching glyph.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog over cmplog and the technique delta is value_profile.
The winner resolved in all 10 trials while the loser blocked in all 10 trials.
The blocker is the dotted-circle true branch inside the Gurmukhi case after a codepoint match.
Reaching the matched true side requires codepoint 0x0A72 followed by one of 0x0A3F/0x0A40/0x0A47 in the Gurmukhi arm.
The winner uniquely entered the 0x0A72 arm that the loser never reached.
The winner took the blocker true side while the loser never did, despite the loser iterating the loop far more.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 162  T=1 F=13  T=0 F=300  if (matched) _output_with_dotted_circle (buffer);  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile_cmplog adds CMP_MAP gradient buckets on top of the cmplog instrumentation.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
union of cmplog and value_profile — edge counters,
per-execution CMP buffer (`CmpLogObserver`), and CMP_MAP gradient buckets.

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog's feedback is the same edge-bucket signal as naive, with no value-profile gradient.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
same edge-bucket signal as naive. The CMP buffer is
consumed by the mutator, not by feedback.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (value_profile delta), not I2SRandReplace, so lineage dash-row verification is not the applicable signal; both involved fuzzers carry I2SRandReplace, so a dash row would not discriminate the pair. The discriminating evidence is the source/divergent-branch overlay showing the winner uniquely enters the exact-codepoint 0x0A72 arm (L144 W T=1 vs L T=0).

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness with the same exact two-codepoint Gurmukhi gate in which value_profile_cmplog and cmplog resolve the matched branch at statistically indistinguishable rates, showing the CMP_MAP gradient gives no advantage over edge-only feedback for this comparison.

weakest_evidence_point: No seed bytes are attributed to the winning value_profile_cmplog provenance for the matched pair, so the byte-level proof that the winner contained 0x0A72-followed-by-vowel is inferred from the per-role branch hit counts rather than directly read from a resolving seed.
